Title: Compliance Data and Business Intelligence Analyst
Description:
Are you passionate about protecting the environment? Do you go out of your way to reduce, reuse, and recycle? Do you envision a waste-free future?

If you answered yes to those questions, then we’d like to hear from you.

We are the Resource Productivity and Recovery Authority (RPRA), Ontario’s circular economy regulator.




Our vision is a waste-free and prosperous Ontario. And, our mission is to support waste reduction and resource recovery by enforcing compliance with Ontario’s producer responsibility laws, providing registry services to our regulated community, and reporting publicly on Ontario’s progress towards a circular economy.




We are looking for a talented and committed individual to join us as Compliance Data and Business Intelligence Analyst to support the government’s efforts to protect the environment and advance a new economy in which waste is reused, recycled, and reintegrated.




Job Summary




The Compliance Data & Business Intelligence Analyst supports the organization’s circular economy mandate by delivering high-quality analysis, analytics, reporting, and data stewardship across regulated resource recovery programs (including the Blue Box, tires, and all other RRCEA programs). You will need to have strong knowledge of compliance, policy, regulatory, and market frameworks, along with a deep understanding of waste management planning and operations across relevant sectors; specific knowledge of the Blue Box program is a strong asset. Using policy analysis and research methodologies, you will assess compliance trends, interpret regulatory requirements, and support evidence‑based decision‑making and program performance monitoring. Working closely with internal teams (e.g., Compliance, IT, Data Analytics, Finance) and external stakeholders (e.g., service providers, producers, the Ministry), you will ensure compliance data integrity, usability, and actionability in support of evidence-based decision-making, regulatory oversight, and compliance program performance monitoring.




Essential Duties and Responsibilities:




1) Data-Driven Compliance Decision Making

Develop processes and maintain regular compliance reporting, including the development of reporting procedures and policies, across regulated programs and operational areas, ensuring clarity, accuracy, and consistency.
Assist with cultivating data literacy in compliance staff, provide guidance and training regarding data tools and analytics; assess and address compliance data requests.
Produce deliverables such as operational performance summaries, risk assessment reports, and presentations that synthesize data trends, risks, and compliance recommendations.
Assist with the development and monitoring of KPIs and corporate performance measures to assess program effectiveness and identify continuous improvement opportunities.
Ability to support inspector workflows is required.




2) Data Stewardship & Quality Assurance

Act as a data steward for priority datasets, defining and maintaining:
Compliance data definitions and rules
Standardized reporting logic
Data quality controls and exception handling
Documentation and audit trails for repeatable reporting
Complete regular case data reviews, assessing case data against internal compliance procedures and policies and reporting on review outcomes to compliance leadership and in executive reports.
Support data governance activities including data request management, controlled access, and appropriate handling of sensitive or confidential information.
Support cross-functional projects such as registry improvements, public communications and reports, budgeting and fee setting, and systems/process enhancements.




3) Resource Recovery Program Analytics

Provide resource recovery data analytics support for all RRCEA programs, with a focus on Blue Box.
Support comparative analysis across jurisdictions (where relevant), and identify implications for program design, compliance approaches, or operational enhancements.
Engage with the Ministry, registrants and stakeholders to understand data needs and assess feasibility while considering RPRA’s Access and Privacy Code and resourcing.




4) Other Duties

Perform other related duties as assigned.




Job Qualifications




Education

Post-secondary education in business, data, finance, public policy, environmental studies, or other related disciplines or equivalent work experience.




Experience

At least 4 years’ progressive experience in business intelligence, data analytics, reporting, or program analytics (experience in regulated environments strongly preferred).
Demonstrable knowledge of EPR regulatory frameworks, municipal waste analysis, waste management or related sectors.
Prior compliance/regulatory experience is an asset.




Skills, Knowledge & Competencies

Sound knowledge of waste management planning and operations, both broadly and applicable to specific waste management sectors; specific knowledge in the Blue Box program an asset.
Sound knowledge of policy analysis and research methodology.
Strong ability to analyze and interpret quantitative and qualitative data to produce actionable recommendations.
Advanced proficiency working with: Excel, CRM data (Salesforce), Data visualizations.
Strong organization, prioritization, and multitasking skills in a complex, deadline-driven environment; thrives in a climate of change.
Strong written and verbal communication skills.
Knowledge and understanding of the RRCEA, WDTA, associated regulations and continued program and stewardship plans an asset.
Ability to understand and interpret financial and operational reports required under RPRA’s mandate.
High attention to detail and accuracy; strong judgment and discretion with confidential data.
Customer-service mindset and collaborative approach to stakeholder relationships.
Project coordination skills (planning, tracking, reporting, risk/issue logs).
Comfort working with compliance-focused data and risk analysis.
Ability and willingness to be trained and designated as an inspector under the RRCEA; compliance/regulatory experience is an asset.
Ability to communicate in French an asset.




Travel Requirements

Occasional travel within Ontario




Physical Demands

Office/Desk Job




Working with the Resource Productivity and Recovery Authority

You will be a part of a collaborative team doing ground-breaking and meaningful work with a critical environmental and economic mission.

This is a full-time permanent role, working Monday to Friday from 9:00 a.m. to 5:00 p.m., with flexibility as needed. Our highly attractive total compensation plan includes a competitive salary (commensurate with experience), health benefits, personal days, and three weeks of vacation to start.

We have a hybrid work arrangement and support flexible work schedules. Remote work is expected to be completed from a location within Ontario. Our office is conveniently located in North York on the Yonge subway line at Sheppard Avenue.

RPRA is committed to a professional, respectful work environment where employee safety and well-being are prioritized and harassment of any kind is not tolerated.

We strive to build a team that reflects the diversity of the community we work in and encourage applications from traditionally underrepresented groups such as women, visible minorities, Indigenous peoples, people identifying as LGBTQ2SI, veterans, and people with disabilities.

RPRA welcomes and encourages applications from people with disabilities. Accommodations are available on request for candidates taking part in all aspects of the selection process.

The Resource Productivity and Recovery Authority utilizes artificial intelligence (AI) to screen and assess applicants for this position based on pre-defined, job-related criteria. AI acts as a supporting tool, but all final decisions are made by human HR professionals and hiring managers.




Target salary range: Min: $$84,222 - $105,278




How to apply

Submit your cover letter and resume (Name_JobTitle_2026) with the Subject Line: Name -JobTitle - 2026 at: careers@rpra.ca

Title: Data Analyst, Circular Material Innovations
Description:
About The Team

The Circular Material Innovations (CMI) team is part of Product Stewardship at Canadian Tire. The team manages packaging data, regulatory reporting, and waste diversion programs (e.g., tires, packaging, IC&I waste) across Canada. It also identifies and delivers circular business and operational opportunities. The team is tasked with improving data quality and infrastructure, and identifying tangible opportunities to reduce cost, risk, and environmental impact.

Ideal Candidate Profile

The Data Analyst, Circular Material Innovations is a technically strong, detail-oriented problem solver who owns the data accuracy, logic and defensibility of packaging and stewardship data. They can create the necessary data architecture and systems to ensure a data-driven approach to reporting, compliance, and strategy. They bring technical excellence, analytical judgment, and cross-functional communication skills.

What You'll Do


Own data compilation, validation and logic that is used for regulatory reporting and enterprise decision-making.
Design and build integrated cross-functional data management systems and processes for enterprise priorities such as stewardship programs and vendor fee accuracy.
Coordinate with internal teams and vendors to collect, clarify, validate, and correct data required for compliance, packaging, fee accuracy, and operational reporting.
Maintain and improve data tools, trackers, and reporting processes to strengthen accuracy, reduce manual effort, and support repeatable ways of working.
Analyze packaging, product, vendor, and stewardship data to identify trends, gaps, risks, and opportunities for process or cost improvements.
Support Extended Producer Responsibility, Federal Plastics Registry, Blue Box, tire recycling, and other stewardship reporting by preparing accurate, well-documented data inputs.
Build simple automation, reporting logic, and quality checks that improve consistency and reduce repetitive manual work.


What You Bring


Bachelor’s degree or diploma in Data Analytics, Computer Science, Finance/Accounting, Sustainability, or a related field, or equivalent work experience.
3-6 years' experience as a data coordinator, analyst, or similar analytical role.
Expertise in data analysis tools/software (e.g., Excel, SQL, Knime, Azure, Power BI, etc.).
Familiarity with low-code or automation tools (e.g., workflow tools, macros, RPA, Power Automate).
Keen understanding of how data analytics informs strategic initiatives and regulatory compliance.
Clear communication skills who can explain data findings, risks, and recommendations to technical and non-technical audiences.
Demonstrated ability to validate data, identify inconsistencies, document assumptions, and produce clear, accurate reporting outputs.
Comfortable working with ambiguity, large datasets, multiple stakeholders, and evolving regulatory or business requirements.
Strong organizational skills with the ability to manage deadlines, track details, and keep work structured across multiple priorities.
Experience with retail, product data, packaging, Extended Producer Responsibility, Blue Box, Ontario Tire Recycling, Federal Plastics Registry considered an asset.
Knowledge CTC data systems would be asset.


Our typical hiring range is between $53,000.00 and $88,000.00 CAD Annual. The target salary range is between $65,000.00 and $80,000.00 CAD Annual. Salary decisions are also dependent on other factors such as your experience, job-related knowledge, skills and competencies, market location, industry benchmarks, internal equity and other role-specific requirements.

This posting represents an existing vacancy within our organization.

We may use artificial intelligence tools as part of our recruitment process to assist in the initial screening of resumes. All hiring decisions, including candidate evaluation, selection, and disposition, are made by human recruiters.

About Us

Canadian Tire Corporation, Limited (“CTC”) is one of Canada’s most admired and trusted companies. With more than 90 Owned Brands, over 1,600 retail locations, financial services, exemplary e-commerce capabilities, and exciting market-leading merchandising strategies. We dream big and work as one to innovate with purpose for our customers at every level of our business, investing in new technologies and products, and doubling down on top talent to drive the company forward. We offer competitive salaries and wages to CTC employees, as well as store discounts, supported learning through our Triangle Learning Academy, Canadian Tire Profit Sharing, and retirement and savings programs for eligible employees. As part of our enhanced flex benefits program, we offer mental health benefits in the amount of $5,000 per year for benefits-eligible employees and their families, including total well-being, and mental health tools and resources for all employees. Join us in helping to make life in Canada better through living and working our Core Values: we are innovators and entrepreneurs at our core, outcomes drive us, inclusion is a must, we are stronger together and we take personal responsibility. It is an especially exciting time to join CTC and its family of companies where career opportunities are wide-ranging! Join us, where there's a place for you here.

Our Commitment to Diversity, Inclusion and Belonging

We are committed to fostering an environment where belonging thrives, and diversity, inclusion and equity are infused into everything we do. We believe in building an organizational culture where people are consistently treated with dignity while respecting individual religion, nationality, gender, race, age, perceived ability, spoken language, sexual orientation, and identification. We are united in our purpose of being here to help make life in Canada better.

Accommodations

We stand firm in our Core Value that inclusion is a must. We welcome and encourage candidates from equity-seeking groups such as people who identify as racialized, Indigenous, 2SLGBTQIA+, women, people with disabilities, and beyond. Should you require any accommodation in applying for this role, or throughout the interview process, please make them known when contacted and we will work with you to help meet your needs.

Title: Data & Analytics - BI Developer (Contractor - 12 Months)
Description:
MUFG Investor Services is a trusted partner to many of the world’s largest public and private funds, providing asset servicing and operational solutions built for alternatives. With over $1 trillion in client assets under administration, we offer fund administration, banking, payments, fund financing, foreign exchange overlay, corporate and regulatory services, custody, business consulting, and more. Operating from 17 locations worldwide, we help clients mitigate risk, enhance efficiency, and navigate the operational complexities of today’s investment management landscape. As a division of Mitsubishi UFJ Financial Group (MUFG), one of the world’s largest financial institutions with approximately $3 trillion in assets, we combine deep expertise with the strength and stability of a leading financial institution. To learn more, visit us at www.mufg-investorservices.com.

Job Description

Our Data & Analytics team in MUFG Investor Services (“MUFG”) is looking for a skilled Business Intelligence Developer to join us. The successful candidate will be a key part of the Analytics team that was established as part of the strategic expansion and long-term transformation of the finance function. Their role will be to develop data solutions that enhance the overall performance and quality of finance data.

You Will:


Design and Plan BI Solutions:
Collaborate with stakeholders and team members to understand business needs and translate them into technical specifications.
Design and architect data models, ETL processes, and reporting solutions.
Develop and Implement Solutions:
Write efficient software code using appropriate languages/systems, such as SQL, Scribe, Microsoft SSIS (DAX or MDX), and Microsoft SSRS.
Implement BI solutions based on the Fabric product suite and ensure compliance with enterprise architectural standards.
Actively support the conversion of legacy reporting in excel to new and enhanced views through a scalable reporting solutions.
Data Preparation & Blending: Extract, transform, and load (ETL) data from diverse sources like SQL Server, XML, Excel, and CSV using Alteryx Designer.
Cloud Infrastructure Expertise:
Leverage your hands-on knowledge of AWS-based cloud infrastructure to help drive our next phase of cloud adoption. Support the Solutions architect with analysis and input around technical capabilities of the product. design and deploy BI solutions.
Ensure proper integration with Enterprise Data Warehouse (EDW) and Master Data Management (MDM) platforms and standards.
Testing and Troubleshooting:
Test and troubleshoot Business Intelligence applications to ensure accuracy and reliability.
Document metadata and reporting applications for current and future reference.
Modern BI Solutions:
Design and develop modern BI solutions, including Performance Management Dashboards, Business Analytics Models, and Visualizations.
Provide technical designs and specifications for data integration and storage.
Automate reporting process, including Power Automate or similar tools to automate data upload, report delivery to specific users etc
Lifecycle Support:
Provide full lifecycle support, from implementation to maturity, for BI solutions.

Qualifications

You Have:


Education: A relevant degree such as Bachelor’s or Master’s Degree in Computer Science, Engineering, Business, Information Technology, or related fields.
Certifications: Consider certifications such as PowerBI, SQL Server, Microsoft Fabric, UI/UX development.
Skills:
Proficiency in SQL, Python and experience with BI tools (report builders, ad-hoc queries, OLAP, dashboards, data mining, and predictive modeling).
Experience with ERP (e.g. Workday) and Databricks will be preferred.
Experience with ETL tools such as Alteryx, Azure Data Factory, SSIS
Familiarity with Fabric product suite and cloud-based infrastructure.
Proven abilities to take initiative and be innovative.
Analytical mind with a problem-solving aptitude.

Additional Information

What’s in it for you to join MUFG Investor Services? 

Take a look at our careers site and you’ll find everything you’d expect working with one of the fastest-growing businesses at one of the world’s largest financial groups. Now take another look. Because it’s how we defy expectations that really defines us. You’ll feel that difference in all kinds of ways.  Our vibrant CULTURE. Connected team. Love of innovation, laser client focus. 

So, why settle for the ordinary?  Apply now for your next Brilliantly Different opportunity. 

We thank all candidates for applying; however, only those proceeding to the interview stage will be contacted.

MUFG is an equal opportunity employer.

Title: Senior Marketing Data Analyst (m/f/d)
Description:
We are a Canadian leader in digital automotive solutions. Our flagship brands — AutoTrader.ca, AutoSync, Dealertrack Canada and CMS — help Canadians buy, sell, and finance vehicles with confidence.

AutoTrader.ca is Canada’s largest automotive marketplace, with over 25 million monthly visits. Through AutoSync, we provide software solutions to 3,500+ dealers, streamlining their operations, marketing, and sales. Dealertrack Canada is the country’s top automotive financing portal, processing more than 6.5 million credit applications each year. Collateral Management (CMS) is a national tech solution that boosts lien and registration services, recovery services, and insolvency management solutions for Canadian Lenders

As part of AutoScout24 group, Europe’s largest online car marketplace, we’re shaping the future of automotive retail in Canada and beyond.

AutoScout24 is on a mission to revolutionize how marketing decisions are driven through data and analytics. We are seeking an experienced and motivated Marketing Data Analyst (m/f/d) to take ownership of our marketing analytics framework and help us make data-informed decisions to optimize efficiency and ROI of our multi-channel marketing funnel. This role is perfect for someone who combines strong technical skills with sharp business judgment - and who thrives working close to top management on high-impact, complex analyses.

Our team treats AI as a core working principle. We actively integrate AI tools into our day-to-day workflows to raise the speed, quality, and depth of our analytics work — and we expect this mindset from everyone who joins us.

What you will do


Design and implement scalable data models and pipelines within our Data Lake, to integrate and manage marketing data across channels.
Build and maintain engaging dashboards to monitor demand, user acquisition and marketing efficiency, following best practices in data visualization to deliver clear and actionable insights.
Empower marketing teams with self-service analytics by curating reliable data sources and building automated, scalable data solutions that enable independent, data-driven decision-making.
Work closely with online, app, and offline marketing teams to deliver insights and recommendations that optimize multi-channel efforts, media planning, budget allocation, and user acquisition.
Partner with fellow analysts to evaluate performance across key platforms — including Google Analytics, Adjust, AppsFlyer, Google Ads, Meta, and Criteo — and translate findings into strategy.
Lead exploratory analytics projects — including new KPI development, A/B testing, incrementality studies, offline measurement, and attribution modelling — to address evolving business challenges.
Contribute technical expertise and business judgment to deep-dive analyses and reports for senior and top management, and lead multi-disciplinary projects end-to-end, managing timelines and stakeholder communication.
Leverage AI tools and automation to increase the efficiency, quality, and scale of analytical work — from data preparation and insight generation to reporting and stakeholder communication.


What you will bring


4+ years of experience in marketing analytics, ideally in online marketplaces, B2C or B2B environments, with a strong grasp of online and app marketing, campaign optimization, and budget allocation.
Proficiency in SQL and hands-on experience building data pipelines and ELT workflows using tools like Airflow and dbt, with familiarity across cloud platforms such as BigQuery, Redshift, or Snowflake.
Strong experience with BI and dashboarding tools (Tableau, QuickSight, PowerBI, or similar) and a solid understanding of data visualization best practices.
Deep understanding of Google Analytics, MMPs (Adjust, AppsFlyer), and major ad platforms (Google, Meta, Criteo), including tracking systems and reporting structures.
Proven ability to translate complex data into clear, actionable recommendations for both technical and non-technical audiences, including C-level stakeholders.
Outstanding attention to detail and a strong analytical mindset, with a grasp of statistical concepts to deliver effective and pragmatic solutions.
A proven track record of proactively and independently leading cross-functional analytics initiatives in a collaborative, multi-level organization.
A proactive approach to integrating AI tools into analytical workflows — with genuine curiosity about how AI can improve the speed and quality of data work.
Excellent verbal and written English skills.


Nice to have

Experience with web/app marketing attribution, budget planning, or forecasting models


Amazon QuickSight
Google Search Console
Google Tag Manager


What's in it for you

We understand that there is life at work and life outside of work. Here are a few benefits we all benefit from that support us to be our creative best.

Benefits from Day 1


Gym discounts
Employee and Family Assistance program
Virtual wellness events
Conferences & training budget
Regular internal training programs


Financial planning


Let us help you invest in your future with 3% matching towards your pension and multiple forms of income protection.


Compensation

The base salary range for this position is CAD 100K – CAD 140K.

This range reflects the expected compensation at the time of posting. The final offer may vary and can be higher based on relevant skills, experience, location, and market conditions. Based on the role the total rewards package may also include benefits, bonus, and other employee offerings.

For a career where you can drive our business and shape your future, apply now.

Use of Artificial Intelligence in Hiring: We use artificial intelligence (“AI”) in our hiring process, including to screen, assess, or select applicants for this position.

Vacancy Status: This job posting is for an existing vacancy.

Title: Business Data Analyst
Description:
What You’ll Do

As the Business Data Analyst, you will provide structured transparency and management of our data assets. By focusing on data sourcing, integration, data quality and governance principles, the Business Data Analyst will ensure we know where our data comes from, is stored, is going and how it is being used and transformed along the way. The Business Data Analyst will enable a collaborative data environment where all stakeholders have access to the data they need, understanding, confidence and trust in the data they use and support for the data they steward.


Promote and contribute to the development and continuous improvement of data governance and data management standards, policies, procedures and controls
Continuously assess the current state of our data environment to identify process improvement opportunities such as inefficient table structures, ineffective load processes, R.O.T. or metadata and reference data enhancements
Proactively monitor data, metadata and reference data and support the data stewardship community in resolving identified data quality and data integrity concerns
Maintain data models as well as metadata and documentation such as data glossaries and catalogs, data mappings, data lineage and data classifications
Participate in data integrations as the liaison between I.T., business data owners\stewards and subject matter experts and conduct data profiling and data discovery to support these efforts
Maintain open communication between all data stakeholders, from data producers to consumers, and remain an active enabler of the data governance structure of the Bank


What You Bring


Post-secondary education in Computer Science or related program
Proficient in PL\SQL and confident working in a structured data environment
Exposure to some of the following tools or technology: Oracle, Databricks, IKC, Azure, GCP, Big Query, Hadoop, Hive, Python, R, Shell Scripting, SAS, basic Linux commands
Experience working with data in a corporate environment and supporting cross-functional data governance including monitoring data quality, recommending improvements in data, data modeling, data classification and data governance capabilities
Clear understanding of data management concepts: Big data/Hadoop, data lake, data warehouse, data mart, relational databases, data ingestion/ETL, master data
Analytical mindset and critical thinking skills
Advanced working knowledge of Microsoft tools including Visio, PowerPoint and Excel
Good understanding of emerging data technologies
Experience with data governance or curation platforms considered an asset
CDMP certification considered a strong asset


Location: Oakville, Ontario (Hybrid: In-office 4 days a week)

We’re always looking for great talent! In addition to competitive pay, we offer:


Comprehensive benefits and retirement programs
Performance incentives, Continuing Education Programs
Other perks to support your well-being
Career growth opportunities and product discounts


Broadband Salary Range: $53,000 – $88,000 CAD

Our typical hiring range is between $53,000 and $70,000 CAD. Salary decisions are also dependent on other factors such as your experience, industry benchmarks, internal equity and other role-specific requirements. For critical roles, the compensation offering will be reviewed to ensure alignment with market rate and conditions and the unique value you bring to the role.

Title: Senior Data Analyst – Power BI/Tableau, SQL

Description:
Role: Senior Data Analyst – Power BI/Tableau, SQL

Skills: Digital – BI Data Visualization (Tableau, Microsoft Power BI)

Experience Required: 10 & Above

Role Description / Key Responsibilities

1. Collect, process, and analyze data from various sources (e.g., databases, spreadsheets, APIs).
2. Develop and maintain dashboards and reports using tools like Power BI, Tableau, or Excel.
3. Work with stakeholders to understand data requirements and deliver actionable insights.
4. Monitor and evaluate the effectiveness of existing data processes and recommend improvements.
5. Conduct root cause analysis and present findings to cross-functional teams.
6. Maintain data integrity and quality across all systems and reports.
7. Support business decision-making by collecting, analyzing, and interpreting large datasets to identify trends and patterns, improve operational efficiency, and enable strategic decisions.

Qualifications

1. Bachelor’s degree in Statistics, Mathematics, Computer Science, Economics, or a related field.
2. 4+ years of experience in data analysis or similar role.
3. Strong knowledge of SQL, Excel, and at least one data visualization tool (Power BI, Tableau, etc.).
4. Familiarity with statistical techniques and data modeling.

Skills

1. Strong problem-solving and critical-thinking abilities.
2. Excellent written and verbal communication skills.
3. Ability to present complex information in a clear and concise manner.
4. Attention to detail and high level of accuracy.

4. Monitor and evaluate the effectiveness of existing data processes and recommend improvements.
5. Conduct root cause analysis and present findings to cross-functional teams.
6. Maintain data integrity and quality across all systems and reports.
7. Support business decision-making by collecting, analyzing, and interpreting large datasets to identify trends and patterns, improve operational efficiency, and enable strategic decisions.

Qualifications

1. Bachelor’s degree in Statistics, Mathematics, Computer Science, Economics, or a related field.
2. 4+ years of experience in data analysis or similar role.
3. Strong knowledge of SQL, Excel, and at least one data visualization tool (Power BI, Tableau, etc.).
4. Familiarity with statistical techniques and data modeling.

Skills

1. Strong problem-solving and critical-thinking abilities.
2. Excellent written and verbal communication skills.
3. Ability to present complex information in a clear and concise manner.
4. Attention to detail and high level of accuracy.

Title: Business Intelligence Specialist
Description:
Business Intelligence Senior Advisor – IRP

Location: VPC 500 Consumers Road, North York, ON

Duration: 1 Year

Schedule: Hybrid Model – In-Office (Monday, Tuesday & Thursday), Remote (Wednesday & Friday)




About the Role

We are seeking a Senior Advisor, Business Intelligence to provide advanced data and analytics support for Gas initiatives. This role partners with internal clients to understand business needs, deliver analytical solutions, guide junior analysts, and strengthen data quality standards while advancing analytics capabilities through special projects and continuous improvement.




What You’ll Do

Partner with internal teams to identify strategic business needs and shape customized analytical approaches.
Lead complex analytical initiatives end-to-end, from problem definition to validated recommendations.
Deliver high-quality analytics solutions, reports, and insights aligned with business priorities.
Develop and validate statistical regression and prediction models for forecasting and performance optimization.
Oversee evaluation of data sources, ensuring accuracy and reliability.
Enhance dashboards and reporting solutions for actionable decision-making.
Facilitate brainstorming sessions and workshops to drive innovative analytics applications.
Communicate insights effectively across diverse audiences and organizational levels.
Guide exploratory data analysis to uncover trends and opportunities.
Build and maintain deep business and system knowledge within supported areas.




Who You Are

Degree in Mathematics, Economics, Computer Science, IT, Statistics, or related field (advanced degree is an asset).
7+ years of experience in Business Intelligence, data analytics, or statistical analysis.
Advanced proficiency in SQL, Python, R, and experience with complex data sets.
Skilled in Azure Databricks and modern analytics platforms.
Strong communication skills to translate complex analysis into clear business insights.
Advanced proficiency in Excel, Power BI, Visio, GIS.
Proven ability to collaborate across teams and manage multiple priorities.
Experienced in leading workshops and presenting to stakeholders.




Preferred Assets:

Predictive modelling, forecasting, and statistical analysis.
Knowledge of machine learning techniques.
Experience with data warehousing, data modelling, and cloud-based analytics platforms.




Working Conditions

Typical office environment.
Travel required (approx. 5%, mostly within Ontario).

Title: Data Modeler
Description:
The Data Modeler will support AMI 2.0 analytics program by designing and documenting clear, analytics-ready data models within IBM Netezza. The role focuses on aligning business concepts with data structures to support consistent analytics, reporting, and data sharing across teams. This is a design and enablement role, not an ETL development or platform operations role.




Key Responsibilities:

Perform data discovery on datasets available in IBM Netezza
Design and maintain logical and analytical data models to support AMI 2.0 use cases
Define and align core business entities (e.g., customer, premise, meter, usage, time, grid assets)
Work with business, analytics, and architecture teams to clarify definitions and data requirements
Create and maintain data dictionaries, model documentation, and metadata
Support downstream analytics and reporting needs (e.g., Bidgely, Facts BI) through clear data structures
Assist QA and validation teams by providing data modeling subject-matter support










Required Skills:

Experience in data modeling for analytics or data warehouse environments
Strong understanding of relational and analytical data structures
Ability to translate business requirements into clear data designs
Experience working with enterprise data platforms (IBM Netezza or similar preferred)
Strong documentation and communication skills

Title: Data & Analytics - BI Developer (Contractor - 12 Months)
Description:
MUFG Investor Services is a trusted partner to many of the world’s largest public and private funds, providing asset servicing and operational solutions built for alternatives. With over $1 trillion in client assets under administration, we offer fund administration, banking, payments, fund financing, foreign exchange overlay, corporate and regulatory services, custody, business consulting, and more. Operating from 17 locations worldwide, we help clients mitigate risk, enhance efficiency, and navigate the operational complexities of today’s investment management landscape. As a division of Mitsubishi UFJ Financial Group (MUFG), one of the world’s largest financial institutions with approximately $3 trillion in assets, we combine deep expertise with the strength and stability of a leading financial institution. To learn more, visit us at www.mufg-investorservices.com.





Job Description



Our Data & Analytics team in MUFG Investor Services (“MUFG”) is looking for a skilled Business Intelligence Developer to join us. The successful candidate will be a key part of the Analytics team that was established as part of the strategic expansion and long-term transformation of the finance function. Their role will be to develop data solutions that enhance the overall performance and quality of finance data.



You Will:



Design and Plan BI Solutions:
Collaborate with stakeholders and team members to understand business needs and translate them into technical specifications.
Design and architect data models, ETL processes, and reporting solutions.
Develop and Implement Solutions:
Write efficient software code using appropriate languages/systems, such as SQL, Scribe, Microsoft SSIS (DAX or MDX), and Microsoft SSRS.
Implement BI solutions based on the Fabric product suite and ensure compliance with enterprise architectural standards.
Actively support the conversion of legacy reporting in excel to new and enhanced views through a scalable reporting solutions.
Data Preparation & Blending: Extract, transform, and load (ETL) data from diverse sources like SQL Server, XML, Excel, and CSV using Alteryx Designer.
Cloud Infrastructure Expertise:
Leverage your hands-on knowledge of AWS-based cloud infrastructure to help drive our next phase of cloud adoption. Support the Solutions architect with analysis and input around technical capabilities of the product. design and deploy BI solutions.
Ensure proper integration with Enterprise Data Warehouse (EDW) and Master Data Management (MDM) platforms and standards.
Testing and Troubleshooting:
Test and troubleshoot Business Intelligence applications to ensure accuracy and reliability.
Document metadata and reporting applications for current and future reference.
Modern BI Solutions:
Design and develop modern BI solutions, including Performance Management Dashboards, Business Analytics Models, and Visualizations.
Provide technical designs and specifications for data integration and storage.
Automate reporting process, including Power Automate or similar tools to automate data upload, report delivery to specific users etc
Lifecycle Support:
Provide full lifecycle support, from implementation to maturity, for BI solutions.


Qualifications



You Have:



Education: A relevant degree such as Bachelor’s or Master’s Degree in Computer Science, Engineering, Business, Information Technology, or related fields.
Certifications: Consider certifications such as PowerBI, SQL Server, Microsoft Fabric, UI/UX development.
Skills:
Proficiency in SQL, Python and experience with BI tools (report builders, ad-hoc queries, OLAP, dashboards, data mining, and predictive modeling).
Experience with ERP (e.g. Workday) and Databricks will be preferred.
Experience with ETL tools such as Alteryx, Azure Data Factory, SSIS
Familiarity with Fabric product suite and cloud-based infrastructure.
Proven abilities to take initiative and be innovative.
Analytical mind with a problem-solving aptitude.


Additional Information



What’s in it for you to join MUFG Investor Services? 



Take a look at our careers site and you’ll find everything you’d expect working with one of the fastest-growing businesses at one of the world’s largest financial groups. Now take another look. Because it’s how we defy expectations that really defines us. You’ll feel that difference in all kinds of ways.  Our vibrant CULTURE. Connected team. Love of innovation, laser client focus. 



So, why settle for the ordinary?  Apply now for your next Brilliantly Different opportunity. 



We thank all candidates for applying; however, only those proceeding to the interview stage will be contacted.



MUFG is an equal opportunity employer.

Title: Analyst, Real Estate Investments
Description:
OPPORTUNITY

Reporting to the Managing Director, Investments, the successful candidate will fill the full time position of Analyst, Real Estate Investments in Toronto. The primary responsibility of this role is to support the acquisition and disposition of a wide range of core, development, and value-add investments in major Canadian cities.




KEY RESPONSIBILITIES

Assist in the development, interpretation, and communication of financial models underpinning new and existing land acquisitions related to future development and construction projects.
Support the preparation of valuation models for ground-up development projects or repositioning of existing properties (including working with a cross-functional team to compile and analyze operational assumptions, development costs, market research, risk & returns analysis and other topics as required to develop a strong business case for each proposed project).
Provide key strategic insights through the use of quantitative and qualitative data (on a location and market-level basis) to inform land acquisitions, development, and project repositioning.
Improve upon current, and develop new, tools and templates to streamline and standardize the project evaluation process.
Present findings in clear, compelling, and professional presentations to internal and external stakeholders, detailing development feasibility, opportunities, risks, etc.
Actively track and report on the progress, timing, and updates on all pipeline projects.
Participate in various internal development and investment related meetings.
Provide a wide-range of financial and real estate focused analytical support as.




Requirements

KEY QUALIFICATIONS

The ideal candidate is highly organized with a keen eye for detail, strong financial acumen, a high level of business maturity and solid decision-making skills
University degree in architecture, planning, real-estate development, or business / finance with a real estate emphasis
The ideal individual will possess 3 years of varied real estate experience
Experience in preparing detailed and professional investment recommendation presentations / reports.
Strong Microsoft Office (Word, Excel, and PowerPoint) skills.
Must be self-starter, a strategic thinker and have a service-oriented attitude that will encourage continuous improvement of service and performance.
Strong leadership and interpersonal skills with an outstanding level of integrity that will gain the trust and respect of staff and external stakeholders.
Demonstrated organizational skills, and the ability to effectively prioritize in a demanding, high paced environment.
Ability to formally articulate and present data as requested.




CORE COMPETENCIES

Being Authentic -Inspiring trust and being courageous.
Focusing on Performance -Being accountable and driving results.
Understanding Stakeholders - Knowing the needs of those who rely on you.
Building Effective Relationships - Collaboration and communication.




PAY RANGE

The expected base pay range for this position is: $85,000 to $100,000.




LOCATION

[Toronto - In Office]

Fengate is an equal opportunity employer, we strive to attract and retain a diverse workforce and are committed to promoting diversity, equity, inclusion, and belonging in the workplace. It is a core priority at Fengate to create, operate with, and continuously grow and sustain an inclusive culture that respects and connects the diversity of our team, our clients, our partners, and the communities we work in.

We are committed to providing accommodation for persons with disabilities. If you require accommodation, we will work with you to meet your needs.

We thank all applicants for their interest in this position; however only candidates selected for an interview will be contacted.

Title: Associate Data & Reporting Analyst (12 Month Term)
Description:
Company: CGIC

Department: Retail Sales

Employment Type: Temporary Full-Time (12 months)

Work Model: Hybrid (2 days in office)

Language: English is required, French is an asset.

Additional Information: This/these role(s) is/are currently vacant

The Opportunity

We are a leading Canadian financial services co-operative committed to being a catalyst for a sustainable and resilient society and our team is essential to deliver on this strategy. That’s why we prioritize our people, to ensure we provide a strong culture and development opportunities which enables our team to thrive and to live our purpose. The best part is that you will work with people that care passionately about you, our clients and our communities.

The Associate Data & Reporting Analyst contributes by enhancing and maintaining the ongoing operational and BAU tasks. You design (with support) and autonomously deliver data and reporting solutions that will help drive operational efficiency by ongoing examination of data and reporting workflows. You understand insurance business domain and ensure your own tasks contribute to meeting the business objectives. You communicate findings to colleagues and managers in an effective manner. With guidance from your manager, you communicate findings to a business audience.

How You Will Create Impact


Execute regularly scheduled operational and BAU tasks and processes
Develop and/or update data and reporting process documentation
Monitor, document and respond to ad-hoc and support requests and ensure the issue and resolution are accurate, complete and to the client’s satisfaction.
Conduct thorough testing and reconciliation practices on data and metrics
Support in the development of new data and reporting workflows with guidance from senior peers
Able to work with stakeholders to gather and document requirements for Data, Dashboard, and reporting needs.



To Join Our Team


You have experience with descriptive analytics tools and languages (MicroStrategy, PowerBI, Excel, SQL, SAS, Python, R, etc)
General knowledge of business operations tools (SalesForce, PolicyCentre, ClaimCentre, BillingCentre) is an asset.
You have a post-secondary education.
Strong communication, time management, problem solving and attention to detail skills.
General P&C knowledge, specifically for home/property insurance is an asset.
This position primarily works with majority non-francophone groups, and teams located outside of Québec, and requires proficiency in English. The essential non-French duties are not assignable to adjacent or other team members. 



How You Will Succeed


You influence change and are committed to continuous improvement, in order to exceed client expectations.

Title: Data Analyst, Circular Material Innovations
Description:
About The Team

The Circular Material Innovations (CMI) team is part of Product Stewardship at Canadian Tire. The team manages packaging data, regulatory reporting, and waste diversion programs (e.g., tires, packaging, IC&I waste) across Canada. It also identifies and delivers circular business and operational opportunities. The team is tasked with improving data quality and infrastructure, and identifying tangible opportunities to reduce cost, risk, and environmental impact.

Ideal Candidate Profile

The Data Analyst, Circular Material Innovations is a technically strong, detail-oriented problem solver who owns the data accuracy, logic and defensibility of packaging and stewardship data. They can create the necessary data architecture and systems to ensure a data-driven approach to reporting, compliance, and strategy. They bring technical excellence, analytical judgment, and cross-functional communication skills.

What You'll Do


Own data compilation, validation and logic that is used for regulatory reporting and enterprise decision-making.
Design and build integrated cross-functional data management systems and processes for enterprise priorities such as stewardship programs and vendor fee accuracy.
Coordinate with internal teams and vendors to collect, clarify, validate, and correct data required for compliance, packaging, fee accuracy, and operational reporting.
Maintain and improve data tools, trackers, and reporting processes to strengthen accuracy, reduce manual effort, and support repeatable ways of working.
Analyze packaging, product, vendor, and stewardship data to identify trends, gaps, risks, and opportunities for process or cost improvements.
Support Extended Producer Responsibility, Federal Plastics Registry, Blue Box, tire recycling, and other stewardship reporting by preparing accurate, well-documented data inputs.
Build simple automation, reporting logic, and quality checks that improve consistency and reduce repetitive manual work.


What You Bring


Bachelor’s degree or diploma in Data Analytics, Computer Science, Finance/Accounting, Sustainability, or a related field, or equivalent work experience.
3-6 years' experience as a data coordinator, analyst, or similar analytical role.
Expertise in data analysis tools/software (e.g., Excel, SQL, Knime, Azure, Power BI, etc.).
Familiarity with low-code or automation tools (e.g., workflow tools, macros, RPA, Power Automate).
Keen understanding of how data analytics informs strategic initiatives and regulatory compliance.
Clear communication skills who can explain data findings, risks, and recommendations to technical and non-technical audiences.
Demonstrated ability to validate data, identify inconsistencies, document assumptions, and produce clear, accurate reporting outputs.
Comfortable working with ambiguity, large datasets, multiple stakeholders, and evolving regulatory or business requirements.
Strong organizational skills with the ability to manage deadlines, track details, and keep work structured across multiple priorities.
Experience with retail, product data, packaging, Extended Producer Responsibility, Blue Box, Ontario Tire Recycling, Federal Plastics Registry considered an asset.
Knowledge CTC data systems would be asset.


Our typical hiring range is between $53,000.00 and $88,000.00 CAD Annual. The target salary range is between $65,000.00 and $80,000.00 CAD Annual. Salary decisions are also dependent on other factors such as your experience, job-related knowledge, skills and competencies, market location, industry benchmarks, internal equity and other role-specific requirements.

This posting represents an existing vacancy within our organization.

We may use artificial intelligence tools as part of our recruitment process to assist in the initial screening of resumes. All hiring decisions, including candidate evaluation, selection, and disposition, are made by human recruiters.

About Us

Canadian Tire Corporation, Limited (“CTC”) is one of Canada’s most admired and trusted companies. With more than 90 Owned Brands, over 1,600 retail locations, financial services, exemplary e-commerce capabilities, and exciting market-leading merchandising strategies. We dream big and work as one to innovate with purpose for our customers at every level of our business, investing in new technologies and products, and doubling down on top talent to drive the company forward. We offer competitive salaries and wages to CTC employees, as well as store discounts, supported learning through our Triangle Learning Academy, Canadian Tire Profit Sharing, and retirement and savings programs for eligible employees. As part of our enhanced flex benefits program, we offer mental health benefits in the amount of $5,000 per year for benefits-eligible employees and their families, including total well-being, and mental health tools and resources for all employees. Join us in helping to make life in Canada better through living and working our Core Values: we are innovators and entrepreneurs at our core, outcomes drive us, inclusion is a must, we are stronger together and we take personal responsibility. It is an especially exciting time to join CTC and its family of companies where career opportunities are wide-ranging! Join us, where there's a place for you here.

Our Commitment to Diversity, Inclusion and Belonging

We are committed to fostering an environment where belonging thrives, and diversity, inclusion and equity are infused into everything we do. We believe in building an organizational culture where people are consistently treated with dignity while respecting individual religion, nationality, gender, race, age, perceived ability, spoken language, sexual orientation, and identification. We are united in our purpose of being here to help make life in Canada better.

Accommodations

We stand firm in our Core Value that inclusion is a must. We welcome and encourage candidates from equity-seeking groups such as people who identify as racialized, Indigenous, 2SLGBTQIA+, women, people with disabilities, and beyond. Should you require any accommodation in applying for this role, or throughout the interview process, please make them known when contacted and we will work with you to help meet your needs.

Title: Chief Technology Officer
Description:
Chief Technology Officer (CTO)

Location: Toronto, Ontario (Hybrid) or Montréal, Québec (Hybrid)

Reports to: CEO

Level: Executive / C-Suite

About us:

Trolley is the payouts platform for the internet economy. Our mission is to unlock the global economic opportunity of the internet by building a truly borderless payouts ecosystem.

Through our platform and API, Trolley enables businesses to pay people anywhere in the world, supporting creators, on demand workers, and suppliers at scale. Companies rely on Trolley as mission-critical infrastructure to automate payouts, collect tax and banking information, and reduce fraud and risk, all in one place.

Today, Trolley is the trusted payouts solution for hundreds of businesses, including Canva, Soundcloud, and Envato. We’ve processed millions of payments globally, helping creators, artists, and suppliers get paid quickly, securely, and with confidence.

As Trolley enters its next stage of growth, we will be heavily investing in AI to accelerate product innovation, improve operational efficiency, and transform how software is built across the company. We are seeking a Chief Technology Officer (CTO) to lead our technology organization and help define how AI shapes the future of Trolley.

The Opportunity

The CTO will be a key member of Trolley's executive leadership team, responsible for defining and executing the company's technology strategy while ensuring the scalability, security, reliability, and innovation of our platform.

A critical mandate for the successful candidate will be driving AI adoption across the engineering team and broader company. We are looking for a leader who has successfully implemented AI powered development practices, enabled organization wide adoption, and delivered measurable improvements in engineering productivity, software quality, and speed of execution.

The CTO will oversee Engineering and Information Security while partnering closely with Product, Operations, Finance, Compliance, and GTM leaders to align technology investments with company objectives.

What You’ll Do

Define and Execute Trolley's Technology Strategy


Define and execute Trolley's long term technology vision and roadmap.
Partner with the CEO and executive leadership team to align technology investments with company strategy and growth objectives.
Ensure the platform remains scalable, secure, reliable, and capable of supporting Trolley's continued global expansion.
Evaluate emerging technologies and identify opportunities to create competitive advantage and improve operational efficiency.
Lead architectural decisions that support long term product innovation and business growth.


Lead AI Transformation Across the Organization


Define and execute Trolley's AI strategy across Engineering and the broader organization.
Drive adoption of AI powered software development practices, coding assistants, testing automation, QA automation, observability, and operational tooling.
Establish measurable improvements in engineering productivity, software quality, and delivery velocity through AI.
Identify opportunities to leverage AI within products, customer experiences, internal operations, and decision making processes.
Build a culture of continuous learning and responsible AI adoption.
Serve as Trolley's executive leader for AI innovation.


Lead and Scale Engineering


Build, develop, and lead a high performing engineering team.
Establish a culture of accountability, ownership, collaboration, and technical excellence.
Recruit, mentor, and retain exceptional engineering leaders and talent.
Implement scalable engineering processes, operating rhythms, and performance metrics.
Ensure consistent delivery of secure, reliable, and high quality software.


Oversee Information Security


Lead Trolley's Information Security strategy and function, with the Head of InfoSec.
Ensure strong security, privacy, and risk management practices across the organization.
Maintain robust cybersecurity controls, incident response capabilities, and business continuity planning.
Partner with Compliance and Risk teams to support customer requirements, audits, certifications, and regulatory obligations.
Foster a security first culture while enabling innovation and speed.


Executive Leadership


Serve as a trusted advisor to the CEO and Board on technology, AI, cybersecurity, and innovation.
Contribute to company wide strategic planning and operational excellence.
Represent Trolley with customers, partners, investors, and industry stakeholders when appropriate.
Help shape the future direction of the company as a member of the executive leadership team.


What We're Looking For

Required Experience


10+ years of progressive technology leadership experience, including experience leading large engineering organizations.
Experience as a CTO, VP Technology, VP Engineering, SVP Engineering, or equivalent senior technology executive within a high-growth SaaS, fintech, payments, marketplace, or technology company.
Demonstrated success implementing AI powered engineering practices and driving meaningful adoption across software development teams.
Proven track record of delivering measurable productivity, quality, and execution improvements through AI enabled development.
Deep understanding of modern software architecture, cloud infrastructure, distributed systems, and platform scalability.
Experience leading technology organizations through periods of significant growth and organizational change.
Experience overseeing Information Security programs and cybersecurity strategy.
Strong track record of recruiting, developing, and retaining world-class technical talent.


Preferred Experience


Experience in fintech, payments, financial infrastructure, or regulated software environments.
Experience leading AI transformation initiatives beyond engineering teams.
Familiarity with modern AI development tooling, agentic workflows, automation platforms, and emerging AI technologies.
Experience scaling globally distributed platforms and engineering teams


What we offer:


Join an amazing team of diverse people working together on a common mission.
Competitive salary & annual Leadership bonus
ESOP Program: Meaningful equity and ownership that aligns your success with ours
Extended healthcare benefits from Day 1.
4 weeks paid vacation per year + public holidays (+ 1 extra vacation day per year of service).
30 days remote work from anywhere.
Gym membership allowance.
1/2 day Fridays off during summer.
Company-provided use of Apple MacBook Pro and Mac gear.

Title: Executive Assistant, Governance & Policy Co-ordinator
Description:
Company Description The Ontario Stone, Sand and Gravel Association (OSSGA) is a non-profit industry association representing more than 280 aggregate producers, equipment suppliers, and service providers across Ontario. Its members supply most of the approximately 164 million tonnes of crushed stone, sand, and gravel used annually to build and maintain the province’s infrastructure. OSSGA collaborates with government, stakeholders, and the public to advance a safe, sustainable, and competitive aggregate industry. The association is committed to supporting responsible resource development and strengthening communities across Ontario. Team members contribute to policy, advocacy, and programs that have tangible impact on infrastructure and the environment.

Role Description The Executive Assistant, Governance & Policy Coordinator is a full-time, on-site role based in Mississauga, ON. This position provides executive support to senior leadership, including managing calendars, scheduling meetings, preparing correspondence, and organizing travel and expense reports. The role supports governance activities by coordinating board and committee meetings, preparing agendas and minutes, managing documentation, and maintaining accurate records and action logs. The coordinator also assists with policy and advocacy work by conducting basic research, organizing briefing materials, tracking regulatory developments, and supporting preparation of reports and presentations. Day-to-day responsibilities include general administrative support, maintaining files and databases, coordinating internal and external communications, supporting events, and liaising with members and partners to ensure timely follow-up and smooth operations.

Qualifications

Strong executive administrative assistance skills, including calendar management, meeting coordination, and document preparation.
Experience providing executive support, including handling confidential information, prioritizing requests, and supporting senior leadership.
Effective communication skills, both written and verbal, with the ability to interact professionally with board members, staff, and external stakeholders.
Solid administrative assistance capabilities, including records management, data entry, and use of standard office software (e.g., MS Office, videoconferencing tools).
Strong organizational skills, attention to detail, and ability to manage multiple priorities and deadlines in a fast-paced environment.
Experience supporting governance processes (e.g., board and committee logistics, minute taking, agenda packages) is an asset.
Post-secondary education in administration, public policy, business, or a related field, or equivalent combination of education and experience.
Familiarity with non-profit or association environments and interest in policy, government relations, or the aggregate/Infrastructure sector is considered an advantage.

Title: Junior Report Developer (Intern), Analytics and Insights
Description:
Company Description

At The Princess Margaret Cancer Foundation (PMCF), our vision is to create a world free from the fear of cancer. Cancer remains the number one cause of death for Canadians and our top priority is to accelerate cancer research and care.

Our role is to garner support for Princess Margaret Cancer Centre, one of the world’s leading cancer research and treatment centres, through philanthropy, fundraising events, and our world-renowned lottery program. The work we accomplish drives groundbreaking research, relentless innovation, and a deep commitment to improving the lives of cancer patients and their loved ones.

Now, we have an opportunity to blaze an even brighter future for everyone affected by cancer.

We will Carry The Fire for cancer patients everywhere, lighting the way forward through discovery, reigniting our commitment to transform cancer outcomes and ease the profound suffering cancer causes patients and their loved ones.

At the PMCF, every one of us carries the fire for someone else. Join us as we inspire a movement and help us achieve our vision to change the way we understand, diagnose, and treat cancer.

Job Description

Union: Non-Union

Department: Customer and Business Operations

Reports to: Manager, Business Intelligence Reporting & Development

Hours: 35 hours

Hourly Rate: $22.50/hour

Vacancy: Existing Vacancy - Backfill

Status: Temporary, Full Time (12 months contract)

Posted Date: June 19, 2026

Closing Date: July 2, 2026

We are seeking a Junior Report Developer (Intern) who is passionate about utilizing data and analytics to help us deliver on our mission. In this role you will support the design, development, and optimization of Power BI dashboards and reports that provide insight into our fundraising programs, operations, and supporter engagement.

This role is ideal for a recent graduate who is curious, detail-oriented, and eager to learn. You enjoy solving problems with data, building reports that uncover trends, and collaborating with others to bring clarity and insight to complex questions.

You Will


Collaborate with business stakeholders to understand requirements and translate them into dashboards that answer key business questions.
Design, develop, and maintain Power BI dashboards and reports that are visually compelling, performant, and user-friendly.
Support the Manager of Reporting & Development in delivering key performance metrics, forecasting models, and measurement frameworks.
Collaborate with Data Engineer to create standardized data views in the data warehouse that integrate multiple data sources (CRM, MailChimp, Finance) and maintain clear, up-to-date documentation outlining data definitions, transformation logic, source mappings, and usage guidelines to support consistency and knowledge sharing across teams.
Design, build, and test data models tailored for dashboard performance and scalability.
Maintain and monitor Power BI data gateways to ensure reliable and timely data refreshes, while troubleshooting connectivity issues in collaboration with stakeholders
Conduct data validation and quality assurance checks to ensure accuracy and reliability of reports.
Ensure reports are well-documented, version-controlled, and follow a consistent design and naming convention.



Qualifications


Recent graduate of an undergraduate or graduate program, preferably in computer science, mathematics, engineering, or related field is mandatory.
Experienced with SQL – able to write structured and efficient queries.
Experience building dashboards using Power BI (or equivalent tool) and creating calculated fields and measures using DAX.
Proficiency in optimizing large datasets and refining slow-performing queries and reports to improve efficiency and responsiveness in Power BI
Proficiency with Python considered an asset – understanding of core programming principles, able to transform data, and maintain automation scripts and internal utility libraries.
Familiar with Git – able to version control scripts and collaborate using branching and pull requests.
Strong documentation skills – able to clearly capture business processes and code logic to support collaboration and future maintenance.
Attention to detail and commitment to data accuracy and presentation.
Strong communication skills, both written and verbal.
Ability to shape and develop requirements into practical solutions.



Additional Information

We believe that the way we work together is just as important as what we accomplish. By making choices and taking actions that align with our values, we achieve greater success, and our work lives become happier and more meaningful.

At PMCF, we strive to foster a culture built on Collaboration, Accountability, Respect, and Excellence (CARE). Central to these values is our commitment to IDEAA: Inclusion, Diversity, Equity, Accessibility, and Anti-Racism.


Experience a sense of purpose that you won’t get anywhere else, in any other job. You will be making a difference for millions of people impacted by cancer
We have an open and approachable culture that enables you to bring your best ideas forward
We offer a hybrid work environment with Tuesday, Thursday and one other day per week in office, or as required subject to business needs

At The Princess Margaret Cancer Foundation (PMCF), our vision is to create a world free from the fear of cancer. Cancer remains the number one cause of death for Canadians and our top priority is to accelerate cancer research and care.

Our role is to garner support for Princess Margaret Cancer Centre, one of the world’s leading cancer research and treatment centres, through philanthropy, fundraising events, and our world-renowned lottery program. The work we accomplish drives groundbreaking research, relentless innovation, and a deep commitment to improving the lives of cancer patients and their loved ones.

Now, we have an opportunity to blaze an even brighter future for everyone affected by cancer.

We will Carry The Fire for cancer patients everywhere, lighting the way forward through discovery, reigniting our commitment to transform cancer outcomes and ease the profound suffering cancer causes patients and their loved ones.

At the PMCF, every one of us carries the fire for someone else. Join us as we inspire a movement and help us achieve our vision to change the way we understand, diagnose, and treat cancer.

Title: Data & Analytics - BI Developer (Contractor - 12 Months)
Description:
MUFG Investor Services is a trusted partner to many of the world’s largest public and private funds, providing asset servicing and operational solutions built for alternatives. With over $1 trillion in client assets under administration, we offer fund administration, banking, payments, fund financing, foreign exchange overlay, corporate and regulatory services, custody, business consulting, and more. Operating from 17 locations worldwide, we help clients mitigate risk, enhance efficiency, and navigate the operational complexities of today’s investment management landscape. As a division of Mitsubishi UFJ Financial Group (MUFG), one of the world’s largest financial institutions with approximately $3 trillion in assets, we combine deep expertise with the strength and stability of a leading financial institution. To learn more, visit us at www.mufg-investorservices.com.

Job Description

Our Data & Analytics team in MUFG Investor Services (“MUFG”) is looking for a skilled Business Intelligence Developer to join us. The successful candidate will be a key part of the Analytics team that was established as part of the strategic expansion and long-term transformation of the finance function. Their role will be to develop data solutions that enhance the overall performance and quality of finance data.

You Will:


Design and Plan BI Solutions:
Collaborate with stakeholders and team members to understand business needs and translate them into technical specifications.
Design and architect data models, ETL processes, and reporting solutions.
Develop and Implement Solutions:
Write efficient software code using appropriate languages/systems, such as SQL, Scribe, Microsoft SSIS (DAX or MDX), and Microsoft SSRS.
Implement BI solutions based on the Fabric product suite and ensure compliance with enterprise architectural standards.
Actively support the conversion of legacy reporting in excel to new and enhanced views through a scalable reporting solutions.
Data Preparation & Blending: Extract, transform, and load (ETL) data from diverse sources like SQL Server, XML, Excel, and CSV using Alteryx Designer.
Cloud Infrastructure Expertise:
Leverage your hands-on knowledge of AWS-based cloud infrastructure to help drive our next phase of cloud adoption. Support the Solutions architect with analysis and input around technical capabilities of the product. design and deploy BI solutions.
Ensure proper integration with Enterprise Data Warehouse (EDW) and Master Data Management (MDM) platforms and standards.
Testing and Troubleshooting:
Test and troubleshoot Business Intelligence applications to ensure accuracy and reliability.
Document metadata and reporting applications for current and future reference.
Modern BI Solutions:
Design and develop modern BI solutions, including Performance Management Dashboards, Business Analytics Models, and Visualizations.
Provide technical designs and specifications for data integration and storage.
Automate reporting process, including Power Automate or similar tools to automate data upload, report delivery to specific users etc
Lifecycle Support:
Provide full lifecycle support, from implementation to maturity, for BI solutions.

Qualifications

You Have:


Education: A relevant degree such as Bachelor’s or Master’s Degree in Computer Science, Engineering, Business, Information Technology, or related fields.
Certifications: Consider certifications such as PowerBI, SQL Server, Microsoft Fabric, UI/UX development.
Skills:
Proficiency in SQL, Python and experience with BI tools (report builders, ad-hoc queries, OLAP, dashboards, data mining, and predictive modeling).
Experience with ERP (e.g. Workday) and Databricks will be preferred.
Experience with ETL tools such as Alteryx, Azure Data Factory, SSIS
Familiarity with Fabric product suite and cloud-based infrastructure.
Proven abilities to take initiative and be innovative.
Analytical mind with a problem-solving aptitude.

Additional Information

What’s in it for you to join MUFG Investor Services? 

Take a look at our careers site and you’ll find everything you’d expect working with one of the fastest-growing businesses at one of the world’s largest financial groups. Now take another look. Because it’s how we defy expectations that really defines us. You’ll feel that difference in all kinds of ways.  Our vibrant CULTURE. Connected team. Love of innovation, laser client focus. 

So, why settle for the ordinary?  Apply now for your next Brilliantly Different opportunity. 

We thank all candidates for applying; however, only those proceeding to the interview stage will be contacted.

MUFG is an equal opportunity employer.

Title: Digital Press Operator (Full Time) - Evening Shift
Description:
ePac, LLC is focused solely on the quick turnaround, short run flexible packaging market. With multiple locations across the United States, Canada, Europe, Australia, and Indonesia. ePac is the first company ever to be developed from the ground up around the latest wide-web digital printing technology.


SUMMARY OF POSITION

The Press Operator efficiently operates, calibrates and maintains an Indigo HP 200K & HP 25K to ensure excellent quality, machine uptime and that customer expectations are exceeded. 

 

ESSENTIAL FUNCTIONS AND RESPONSIBILITIES 

 
Efficiently sets up machine to maximize uptime & loads rolls of material onto press
Regularly calibrates press via color calibration to ensure excellent print quality
Confirms job specifications based on job ticket prior to printing
Monitors print quality while machine is in production
Performs daily and weekly maintenance on press; Notifies Lead Press Operator when equipment needs repair
Opens service calls with HP Technical Team as necessary
Inputs production, consumption, and waste data into Microsoft NAV (ERP)
Supplies printed rolls of film (PE, BOPP, PET etc.) to the lamination department. 
Responsible for machine upkeep, cleanliness and maintenance with Lead Operator or HP Technical Support team’s assistance.
Communicates with Prepress and Converting department when necessary
Support other parts of the operations including pouch assistant, slitting, and lamination, if needed
Keeps pressroom clean and organized
 

EXPERIENCE AND EDUCATION

1+ years of experience in digital printing - HP Indigo presses (HP 20000 & 25000 preferred)
Mechanical & mathematical skills required - Ability to read a ruler
Computer literacy - complete production related documentation on both computer & paper forms
 

BENEFITS 

We offer an excellent benefit and compensation package that consists of the following:

15 days (6%) vacation entitlement & Statutory holidays
Company paid - Health; Dental; Vision;,Life, ADD, Disability
RRSP Plan with generous company match to 3%

Title: Commercial Business Analyst
Description:
Job Description



As a Commercial Business Analyst, your main focus will be to conduct customer segmentation, cohort analysis, and product performance reviews; Act as expert user of SQL and data platforms, supporting data extraction; and provide insights on campaign effectiveness, including conversion attribution and return on ad.



This on-site role reports into our Commercial Manager and is based out of the Toronto Support Office.



A Day in the Life



Working at H&M means no two days are ever the same, but a typical day will include the following responsibilities*



Insights and analytics
Conduct 360 analysis from customer, web and sales perspective to gather insights relevant for Commercial team
Analyze full funnel web performance across .com and app, including traffic, user behavior, product performance, and sales
Provide insights on campaign effectiveness, including conversion attribution and return on ad spend
Analyze sales, customer and web data to draw insights related to QCP, product, assortment and pricing
Plan, support, and evaluate A/B and multivariate testing to optimize customer experience and conversion in online store and CRM/Email
Conduct customer segmentation, cohort analysis, and product performance reviews
Implement tagging, tracking, and SEO analytics to ensure accurate data collection and improve visibility in owned channels
Act as expert user of SQL and data platforms, supporting data extraction and team upskilling
Collaboration
Generate regular reports and dashboards with actionable insights for stakeholders
Support Experience Lead, E-com Leads, Commercial Managers and Marketing team with analytics activities and share insights







Qualifications



Who You Are:



Bachelor’s degree in Business, Marketing, Data Science, Statistics, Economics, or related field
Proficiency in Google Analytics, Adobe Analytics, SQL, Tableau, and Power BI
Strong knowledge of A/B testing methodologies and web tracking implementation
Strong analytical, reporting, and presentation skills
2+ years’ web analytics experience, preferably in eCommerce or fashion retail







Additional Information



Why You’ll Love Working at H&M



We value diverse backgrounds, including but not limited to race, ethnicity, gender, age, religion, sexual orientation, and disability
We pride ourselves on being a values-driven organization, guided by our 7 unique values that have been part of our success story for over 75 years
You’ll have access to our Colleague Resource Groups (CRGs) to find community and networking opportunities among colleagues
You’ll be able to express your personal style with our employee discount at H&M, & Other Stories, and COS





Compensation: salary is $97,200



*This job posting highlights the most critical responsibilities and requirements of the job and is not all-inclusive. There may be additional duties, and responsibilities assigned for this job at the company’s discretion.
